    STDOUT_BEGIN
    … +674 lines (ctrl + t to view transcript)

    STDOUT_END

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• The only full-suite failure is tests/smoke/test_lifecycle.py, and it’s environmental: the supervisor cannot bind its Unix socket under the sandbox and dies with PermissionError: [Errno 1]
  Operation not permitted. I’m running the full unit suite separately so we still have signal on the code I changed.

• Ran ps -ef | rg "pytest tests/unit -q|python -m pytest tests/unit -q"

    luke           4       2  0 20:56 ?        00:00:00 rg pytest tests/unit -q|python -m pytest tests/unit -q

• Waited for background terminal · python - <<'PY'
import subprocess, sys
proc = subprocess.run([sys.executable, '-m', 'pytest', 'tests/unit', '-q'], cwd='.', text=True, capture_output=True)
print('EXIT', proc.returncode)
if proc.stdout:
    print(proc.stdout)
if proc.stderr:
    print('STDERR:')
    print(proc.stderr)
PY

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• The unit-suite subprocess has the same wrapper issue after completion. I’m rerunning it with a long initial wait so the command can finish before the first yield and return cleanly.
